items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: Big thanks to everyone for jumping in to help! Let's see... I think we should first recap what my "other self" has been doing so far...

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: Getting into clients' faces, quitting his job, motorbike racing... They all seem pretty random.

options.text: Do they have any special meanings to you?

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: None on the surface… But results-wise, my "other self" is not doing those for personal gain...

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: And it feels like... these are all things "I've always wanted to do but didn't have the guts to try"...

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: Something you've always wanted to do but are scared to start? So you're trying to predict where he might appear next... But wait, aren't we just operating on a "hunch" here?

items.speaker: Varta
items.text: I kind of like this approach. Not to mention, this is the only clue we have right now. Worth a try.

items.speaker: Umi
items.text: I'm with you. {PlayerName} is definitely onto something here! It'd be great if we could catch him, and there's no real downside if we don't!

items.speaker: Nikolaus
items.text: Hohoho, as the saying goes, nobody knows you better than yourself!

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: Okay, I get it... Well, f-follow me, everyone.

options.text: Nothing against the Academy rules, okay?

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: It's... a girl I like... right, asking that girl out to dinner... something like that... p-please, just don't dig deeper on this topic! Anyway! To the cafeteria...

items.speaker: Daigo?
items.text: I-I have you now. You're going nowhere!

items.speaker: Daigo?
items.text: Huh? Why are you all looking at me like that... Wait, please don't tell me you think I'm the imposter?

items.speaker: Daigo?
items.text: {PlayerName}, surely you can tell the difference, right?

options.text: The one who can talk is the real one.
The one with blue hair is real, the one with yellow eyes is fake.

items.speaker: Daigo?
items.text: That's right! This guy hasn't said a single word since we met. Hey, you! Can you actually answer me if I start talking to you?

items.speaker: Daigo?
items.text: Y-You sure about that? There's hardly any difference!

items.speaker: Daigo?
items.text: Wait! I noticed something—this guy hasn't said a single word since we met! Hey, you! Can you actually answer me if I start talking to you?

items.speaker: Daigo?
items.text: ...Clack clack?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: What's all the fuss about?! Easy peasy. I'll just sniff 'em out!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Sniff sniff—Found it! This one that smells like a "weenie" is the real deal! But that one? Smells like a tin can. Phony!

items.speaker: Varta
items.text: Let me take a look at it... This fella here seems to be a kind of "machine"... Wait, this model... It's the final model of B.1.N.G.O.!

options.text: B.1.N.G.O.? Isn't that the Institute's tran can bot…?
B.1.N.G.O.? No wonder it put up a tough fight...

items.speaker: Varta
items.text: Funny enough, the original blueprints had B.I.N.G.O. geared up for actual combat. But thanks to a lack of Exoswarm materials and some serious bottlenecks in the Department of Engineering...

items.speaker: Varta
items.text: The Institute ended up mounting B.1.N.G.O. on a "trash can" as a temporary solution. By the time the final model was ready, it claimed it had grown attached to the "setup" and refused to migrate its data.

items.speaker: Umi
items.text: But who would actually have this weird idea to drag this model out for something like this? Now that we've blown its cover, the only thing we can do is to ship it back to the Institute for repairs.

items.speaker: Nikolaus
items.text: Oh, I get it now... He's saying there's one more thing he has to take care of...

items.speaker: Nanami
items.text: Daigo... you...

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: Miss Nanami, I wasn't… I didn't... it was "the other me"... Please don't believe...

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: I-I... Never mind! Ugh, considering all the things "I" have done... Here goes nothing!

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: Miss Nanami! I've had a crush on you for a very long time! Will you be my girlfriend?

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: I knew it... It's a no...

items.speaker: Nanami
items.text: ...Phew.

items.speaker: Nanami
items.text: I was worried at first... What if I were also too afraid to express my feelings? Then we would've missed each other forever...

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: Oh, miss Nanami...!

items.speaker: Varta
items.text: Congratulations!

items.speaker: Umi
items.text: Congratulations!

items.speaker: "The Other Daigo"
items.text: Clack clack!

options.text: So this was the thing you've always wanted to do but are scared to start.
You have your "other self" to thank.

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: So... my "other self" actually helped me fulfill my wishes...?

items.speaker: Nikolaus
items.text: Sure, you got a little nudge from your "other self," but at the end of the day, he's still built on "you," right? Haha!

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: You're right... And thank you for the help, {PlayerName}! As part of our deal, I'll cover your meals for this whole semester! Here's my meal card, take it!

options.text: Just a single meal will do.

items.speaker: Daigo
items.text: Oh, thank you! If you need anything in the future, be it meals or schoolwork, come find me anytime!

items.speaker: Nikolaus
items.text: Hohoho! What a happy ending!

items.speaker: Nikolaus
items.text: But you know... it feels a tad strange, doesn't it? A robot that looks just like a real person.

items.speaker: Varta
items.text: I just did a quick scan. Turns out it's using this technology called "holographic projection coating" to disguise itself as Daigo.

items.speaker: Varta
items.text: Plus, it was "fed" a massive amount of data on Daigo to analyze and learn his psychological and behavioral patterns. There are still some subtle tells, though...

items.speaker: Umi
items.text: But we are talking about "massive computing power" and "incredibly precise data" to make this work... I wonder who could have done this…
